title: consensus_vote
description:
Combines cell type predictions from multiple annotation methods into a single consensus prediction using a weighted majority vote. 
For each cell, each method votes for its predicted cell type, optionally weighted by the probability score and/or a per-method weight.
The consensus prediction is the cell type with the highest total weighted vote.

Dataset input.title: Dataset input
Dataset input.description: Dataset input using nf-tower "dataset" or "data explorer". Allows for the input of multiple         parameter sets to initialise a Nextflow channel.

param_list.description: Dataset input can either be a list of maps, a csv file, a json file, a yaml file, or simply a yaml                 blob. The names of the input fields (e.g. csv columns, json keys) need to be an exact match with the workflow input parameters.

input.description: Input h5mu file containing cell type predictions in .obs.

input_obs_predictions.description:
One or more .obs column names containing cell type predictions (labels) from
different annotation methods.

input_obs_probabilities.description:
One or more .obs column names containing prediction probability scores,
one per method in --input_obs_predictions

tie_label.description:
Label to assign when two or more cell types receive equal votes.
If not provided, tied cells are assigned None (missing value).

weights.description: Per-method weights for the consensus vote

output_obs_predictions.description: In which `.obs` slot to store the consensus predicted cell type.

output_obs_score.description:
In which `.obs` slot to store the consensus score, defined as the fraction
of total weight assigned to the winning cell type.

output_compression.description:
Compression format to use for the output AnnData and/or Mudata objects.
By default no compression is applied.

nextflow input-output arguments.title: Nextflow input-output arguments
nextflow input-output arguments.description: Input/output parameters for Nextflow itself. Please note that both publishDir and publish_dir are supported but at least one has to be configured.
